description.text: The S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM) hydroxide adenosyltransferase family groups several fluorinase and chlorinase enzymes whose common feature is that they mediate nucleophilic reactions of their respective halide ions to the C-5' carbon of SAM [[cite:PUB00054078]]. These enzymes utilise a rigorously conserved amino acid side chain triad (Asp-Arg-His) which may have a role in activating water to hydroxide ion. Structural studies indicate that the protein is a homotrimer, with each monomer being composed of N- and C-terminal domains [[cite:PUB00030772], [cite:PUB00048880], [cite:PUB00151506]]. The N-terminal domain has a central seven-stranded β-sheet, which combines parallel and antiparallel strands sandwiched between α-helices. The C-terminal domain forms a β-barrel with a greek-key topology. SAM is bound at the interface between the C-terminal domain of one monomer and the N-terminal domain of the neighbouring monomer, with a total of three molecules bound by the trimer. This group of proteins, which are predominantly found in prokaryotes, used to be known as DUF62 [[cite:PUB00101218]].
This entry represents the N-terminal domain of these enzymes.

PUB00151506.title: Enzyme function prediction using contrastive learning.

PUB00030772.title: Crystal structure and mechanism of a bacterial fluorinating enzyme.

PUB00048880.title: Discovery and characterization of a marine bacterial SAM-dependent chlorinase.

PUB00054078.title: The fluorinase, the chlorinase and the duf-62 enzymes.

PUB00101218.title: An Enzyme Containing the Conserved Domain of Unknown Function DUF62 Acts as a Stereoselective (R<sub>s</sub> ,S<sub>c</sub> )-S-Adenosylmethionine Hydrolase.

overlaps_with.name: S-adenosyl-l-methionine hydroxide adenosyltransferase, N-terminal domain superfamily

representative_structure.name: Crystal structure of a conserved hypothetical protein from Thermus thermophilus HB8
